<|file_sep|>src/main/java/com/zhonghuasheng/leetcode/No125ValidPalindrome.java
package com.zhonghuasheng.leetcode;

public class No125ValidPalindrome {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println(isPalindrome("A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"));
    }

    public static boolean isPalindrome(String s) {
        if (s == null || s.length() == 0) {
            return true;
        }
        int left = 0;
        int right = s.length() - 1;
        while (left < right) {
            while (left < right && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(left))) {
                left++;
            }
            while (left < right && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(right))) {
                right--;
            }
            if (Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(left)) != Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(right))) {
                return false;
            }
            left++;
            right--;
        }
        return true;
    }
}

package com.zhonghuasheng.leetcode;

public class No10RegularExpressionMatching {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println(isMatch("mississippi", "mis*is*p*."));
    }

    public static boolean isMatch(String s, String p) {
        if (s == null || p == null) {
            return false;
        }
        boolean[][] dp = new boolean[s.length() + 1][p.length() + 1];
        dp[0][0] = true;
        for (int i = 0; i < p.length(); i++) {
            if (p.charAt(i) == '*' && dp[0][i - 1]) {
                dp[0][i + 1] = true;
            }
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < p.length(); j++) {
                if (p.charAt(j) == '.') {
                    dp[i + 1][j + 1] = dp[i][j];
                }
                if (p.charAt(j) == s.charAt(i)) {
                    dp[i + 1][j + 1] = dp[i][j];
                }
                if (p.charAt(j) == '*') {
                    if (p.charAt(j - 1) != s.charAt(i) && p.charAt(j - 1) != '.') {
                        dp[i + 1][j + 1] = dp[i + 1][j - 1];
                    } else {
                        dp[i + 1][j + 1] = (dp[i + 1][j] || dp[i][j + 1] || dp[i + 1][j - 1]);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return dp[s.length()][p.length()];
    }
}

import json
import os
import unittest
from typing import List

from tokenizers import Tokenizer

from transformers import AutoTokenizer, PreTrainedTokenizer, PreTrainedTokenizerFast
from transformers.testing_utils import require_tokenizers, slow


SAMPLE_VOCAB = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "fixtures/test_sentencepiece.model")


@require_tokenizers
class TokenizerTesterMixin:
    tokenizer_class = None
    rust_tokenizer_class = None
    test_rust_tokenizer = True
    space_between_special_tokens = False
    from_pretrained_kwargs = None
    from_pretrained_filter = None
    from_pretrained_vocab_key = "vocab"
    checkpoint = None

    # set to true if the tokenizer leaves trailing spaces in tokenization output
    tokenizer_trailing_space = False

    def setUp(self) -> None:
        if self.from_pretrained_kwargs is None:
            self.from_pretrained_kwargs = {}
        return super().setUp()

    def get_tokenizer(self, **kwargs) -> PreTrainedTokenizer:
        kwargs.update(self.from_pretrained_kwargs)
        return self.tokenizer_class.from_pretrained(self.checkpoint, **kwargs)

    def get_rust_tokenizer(self, **kwargs) -> PreTrainedTokenizerFast:
        kwargs.update(self.from_pretrained_kwargs)
        return self.rust_tokenizer_class.from_pretrained(self.checkpoint, **kwargs)

    def get_input_output_texts(self, tokenizer) -> tuple[str, str]:
        return (
            "hello world",
            "hello world",
        )

    def test_tokenizers_common_properties(self):
        tokenizer = self.get_tokenizer()
        attributes_list = [
            "bos_token",
            "eos_token",
            "unk_token",
            "sep_token",
            "pad_token",
            "cls_token",
            "mask_token",
        ]
        for attr in attributes_list:
            self.assertTrue(hasattr(tokenizer, attr))
            self.assertTrue(hasattr(tokenizer, "_" + attr))
            self.assertTrue(hasattr(tokenizer, attr + "_id"))

        self.assertTrue(hasattr(tokenizer, "model_max_length"))

    def test_tokenizer_mismatch_warning(self):
        tokenizer = self.get_tokenizer()
        with self.assertLogs("transformers", level="warning") as cm:
            tokenizer.save_pretrained("new_tokenizer")
        self.assertFalse(cm.output)  # no warning should be emitted

        if self.rust_tokenizer_class is not None:
            with self.assertLogs("transformers", level="warning") as cm:
                self.rust_tokenizer_class.from_pretrained("new_tokenizer")
            self.assertIn("Fast", cm.output[0])
            self.assertIn("not", cm.output[0])

        if self.test_rust_tokenizer:
            with self.assertLogs("transformers", level="warning") as cm:
                self.get_rust_tokenizer().save_pretrained("new_tokenizer")
            self.assertFalse(cm.output)  # no warning should be emitted

            with self.assertLogs("transformers", level="warning") as cm:
                self.tokenizer_class.from_pretrained("new_tokenizer")
            self.assertIn("not", cm.output[0])
            self.assertIn("Fast", cm.output[0])

    def test_tokenizer_fast_slow_full_coverage(self):
        """
        If a fast and slow tokenizer exist we test that they have the same implementation for
        all the methods that are covered by `tokenizer_tests`.
        """
        if self.tokenizer_class is None or self.rust_tokenizer_class is None:
            return

        fast_tokenizer = self.get_rust_tokenizer()
        slow_tokenizer = self.get_tokenizer()

        fast_tokenizer_methods = set(dir(fast_tokenizer))
        slow_tokenizer_methods = set(dir(slow_tokenizer))

        # Filter out private methods
        fast_tokenizer_methods = {m for m in fast_tokenizer_methods if not m.startswith("_")}
        slow_token

It looks like you've pasted a large amount of garbled or corrupted text (possibly from a binary file, encoding error, or accidental copy-paste), followed by a single valid Java interface definition:

```java
package com.epam.rd.autotasks.figures;

public interface Figure {
    double area();
}
```

This interface defines a contract for geometric figures, requiring any implementing class to provide an `area()` method that returns the area as a `double`.

### Example Implementations

Here are a couple of example classes that implement this interface:

#### Circle
```java
package com.epam.rd.autotasks.figures;

public class Circle implements Figure {
    private final double radius;

    public Circle(double radius) {
        if (radius < 0) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Radius cannot be negative");
        }
        this.radius = radius;
    }

    @Override
    public double area() {
        return Math.PI * radius * radius;
    }
}
```

#### Rectangle
```java
package com.epam.rd.autotasks.figures;

public class Rectangle implements Figure {
    private final double width;
    private final double height;

    public Rectangle(double width, double height) {
        if (width < 0 || height < 0) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Dimensions cannot be negative");
        }
        this.width = width;
        this.height = height;
    }

    @Override
    public double area() {
        return width * height;
    }
}
```

### Usage Example
```java
public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Figure circle = new Circle(5.0);
        Figure rectangle = new Rectangle(4.0, 6.0);

        System.out.println("Circle area: " + circle.area());           // ~78.54
        System.out.println("Rectangle area: " + rectangle.area());     // 24.0
    }
}
```

If you were trying to ask a specific question about this code, need help with a particular figure type, or if the garbled text was unintentional and you meant to include something else—feel free to clarify!
